description_long: This object is a cylindrical hand grenade, specifically modeled after a high-explosive (HE) type, with a robust, matte-black metal body and a reinforced base. The upper section features a prominent safety lever and a visible pull ring, both rendered in a dark metallic finish, with yellow accent markings on the lever. The body is adorned with stenciled white text, including the designation 'HMX' and 'HI-EXPLOSIVE GRENADE,' as well as a 40M marking and a warning symbol, indicating its military-grade purpose. The surface shows signs of wear and subtle scratches, suggesting frequent handling or rugged use. The top of the grenade has a segmented cap with a visible set of metallic contacts or teeth, likely part of the arming or detonation mechanism. The overall design is compact and utilitarian, optimized for easy gripping and rapid deployment in urban combat scenarios.
description: A compact, matte-black cylindrical hand grenade with a safety lever, pull ring, and stenciled warning text, designed for high-explosive use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the 40M marking, warning symbol, and the main body details with the safety lever partially visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full length of the safety lever, yellow accent, and partial stenciled text.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the large 'HMX' and 'HI-EXPLOSIVE GRENADE' text, with the lever and cap visible.
description_view_3: Back view with minimal markings, showing the plain cylindrical body and the top cap.
attribute_detonation_mechanism: Detonation is likely initiated by pulling the safety ring and releasing the lever, which would trigger an internal fuse mechanism.
